at_a_glance: You have spent $5.15 across 9 sessions, all in one day. Session 2 was the heavyweight — $1.30 covering all 11 Phase 1 foundation tasks with parallel subagent orchestration. This session was the second most expensive at $0.68, driven mostly by the splash screen feature which required rewriting main.js and creating a new file from scratch. Overall the project is 87% through its planned tasks with only Phase 3 polish remaining.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: The planning session — brainstorm, architect, and spec review for the full app. Expensive relative to output because spec work requires reading everything before writing anything.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: The biggest session by far — all 9 Phase 1 tasks plus the phase gate review. Parallel subagent orchestration ran here, multiplying context costs.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-003: Pure planning — F-STATE feature spec and spec review. No code shipped, but the spec shaped the entire Phase 2 build.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-004: F-STATE build — all 5 state machine tasks. Steady cost at $0.10–0.13 per task, clean implementation session.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-005: F-FIRST-RUN planning and spec review. The add-feature skill reads extensively before writing, driving most of the cost.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-006: Short session — one task (FRN-004) and cost tracking. Lean at $0.20.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-007: Design system implementation plus six visual bug fixes. The dark glass redesign was an L task requiring full CSS rewrite — $0.27 on its own.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-008: DECISION-004 (recording state consolidation) plus BUG-004. Architectural change requiring careful main.js restructure.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-009: Vibrancy diagnosis and fix (BUG-006), thinking state bugs (BUG-007), and the splash screen feature (FEATURE-001). Splash screen drove $0.25 — rewriting main.js and creating splash.html from scratch.

recommendations.title: Zero cache hits across all 9 sessions
recommendations.body: Every session shows cache_read_tokens: 0. CLAUDE.md is large (it now contains 3 active feature sections plus bug fix context) and appears to change frequently as features are added. Splitting CLAUDE.md into a stable core section and a dynamic active-work section would let the stable part cache across turns. Cache reads cost $0.30/MTok vs $3.00/MTok for standard input — a 10× saving on cached content.

recommendations.title: index.html read 5+ times in one session
recommendations.body: This session read index.html for BUG-006, BUG-007-A, BUG-007-B, the diagnostic investigation, and the opacity fix — 5 separate reads of the same ~870-line file. Each full read costs roughly 4,000–5,000 tokens. Batching related fixes into a single session turn (or using targeted grep before full reads) would reduce repeated context loading.

forecast_narrative: Phase 3 has 4 tasks remaining — error state audit, manual smoke test, build verification, and distribution prep — plus the two gate reviews (review: phase 2 and review: final). At the current average of $0.11/task, that is roughly $0.50–0.70 to finish. Fixing prompt caching before Phase 3 could trim $0.10–0.20 off that estimate. Total project looks to land around $5.65–5.85.
